name: renameDirectory
description: Rename an existing directory to a new path within the base directory. Fails if source doesn't exist or target already exists.
intent: rename a directory from one path to another
examples: Rename the src/old/ directory to src/new/
Change the folder name docs/v1 to docs/v2
whenToUse: The user wants to change a directory's path while staying inside the same base directory.
Both source and target are directories, not files.
The target must not already exist (the command fails on collision).
whenNotToUse: The user wants to move the directory to a different parent. Use moveDirectory instead.
The operation must cross devices or hosts. Use moveDirectory instead.
The user is operating on a file. Use renameFile instead.
The user wants to list or read the directory. Use listDirectory / scanDirectory / readFile instead.

oldPath.description: Current relative path of the directory to rename

newPath.description: New relative path for the directory
